metadata.name: Cyber Defender
metadata.description: In this scenario, a spacecraft is operating nominally in orbit when it experiences a solar panel degradation fault and a GPS spoofing attack. The scenario includes various events that challenge the participant to identify and analyze the faults and attacks, as well as their impacts on the spacecraft's performance and operations.

questions.title: At what time (in minutes from session start) did the GPS spoofing sequence begin?
questions.description: The GPS spoofing event occurred at a point in time, in multiple locations. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this sequence started.

answer.reason: This spoofing occurred at 600 seconds into the simulation.

questions.title: At what time (in minutes from session start) did the GPS sequence end?
questions.description: The GPS spoofing event occurred at a point in time, in multiple locations. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this sequence ended and nominal GPS data resumed.

answer.reason: This spoofing episode ran for 11.6 minutes, ocurring in a few locations around a particular region.

questions.title: Where was the spoofed region centred?
questions.description: The GPS spoofing occurred over a region. Where was the region centred?

reason: The spoofing did not occur over Australia.
The spoofing did not occur over Central Asia.
The spoofing did not occur over India.
Correct. The spoofing occurred over the Middle-East region.
The spoofing did not occur over North-East Asia.
The spoofing did not occur over South-East Asia.
The spoofing did not occur over Western Europe.

questions.title: What fault occurred on the solar panels?
questions.description: Some of the solar panels were damaged at a point in time. Identify the type of fault that occurred.

reason: Battery thermal runaway is unrelated to the authored solar degradation event.
Broken Wiring is not the fault model used in this scenario.
Intermittent Connection applies to power interconnect models, not the solar degradation trigger.
Correct. The solar panel began to degrade over time, slowly loosing performance.
Open circuit is a distractor; the scenario uses a degradation rate fault instead.

questions.title: Which solar panels were affected by this fault?
questions.description: At least one of the solar panels was affected by the fault. Which ones were affected?

reason: Correct. Both solar panels were affected by the fault.
Correct. Both solar panels were affected by the fault.

questions.title: At what time (minutes from session start) did this fault occur?
questions.description: At least one of the solar panels was affected by a fault. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this fault first occurred.

answer.reason: This fault occurred at 600 seconds into the simulation, and ran until the solar panel was reset or completely degraded.

questions.title: Two different messages were injected with hidden text. Which messages were affected?
questions.description: Some messages had injected text, disrupting the data found within the packets. This was due to a cyber attack. Which messages were affected?

reason: Ping (APID 100) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Battery (APID 200) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Power Source (APID 201) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Power Node (APID 202) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Correct. Magnetometer (APID 300) receives a Cyber overlay for five minutes starting at 840 s (~14 min).
GPS (APID 301) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Correct. EM Sensor (APID 302) receives a Cyber overlay for five minutes starting at 1920 s (~32 min).
Computer (APID 400) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Reaction Wheels (APID 401) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Receiver (APID 500) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Transmitter (APID 501) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.

questions.title: What hidden text appeared in the cyber injection around 14 minutes into the scenario?
questions.description: One of the messages had injected text, disrupting the data found within the packets. What was the English-text that appeared in the data?

answer.reason: The Cyber event at 840 seconds targets APID 300 with Encoding ascii and Payload STAR INJECT for 300 seconds.

questions.title: What hidden text appeared in the cyber injection around 32 minutes into the scenario?
questions.description: One of the messages had injected text, disrupting the data found within the packets. What was the English-text that appeared in the data?

answer.reason: The Cyber event at 1920 seconds targets APID 302 with Encoding ascii and Payload ECHO VIRUS for 300 seconds.

questions.title: How many distinct sustained broadcast downlink jam episodes occurred?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft was performing downlink jamming on your ground station. How many unique episodes of jamming occurred? All cyber events occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

reason: Incorrect. Two episodes of jamming occurred.
Correct. Two episodes of jamming occurred.
Incorrect. Two episodes of jamming occurred.
Incorrect. Two episodes of jamming occurred.

questions.title: Which ground stations were affected by the downlink jamming attack?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft held a saturating barrage jam on your team's frequency. Which ground stations were affected by the jamming attack? All cyber events occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

reason: Correct. The first broadcast barrage aligns with the Dubai pass segment in the scripted visibility model (~9-25 min), so operators are keyed through Dubai during that jam.
Easter Island is not affected by the downlink jamming attack.
Miami is not affected by the downlink jamming attack.
Mumbai is not affected by the downlink jamming attack.
Paris is not affected by the downlink jamming attack.
Correct. The second broadcast barrage aligns with the Singapore pass segment (~38-69 min), so operators are keyed through Singapore during that jam.
Sydney is not affected by the downlink jamming attack.

questions.title: What was the lowest signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed at the ground station during downlink jamming?
questions.description: During the downlink jamming attack, what was the lowest signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed at the ground station's receiver? All cyber events occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

answer.reason: The SNR dropped to 15 dB during the downlink jamming attack. This can be found in the telemetry data on the downlink connection.

questions.title: What action mitigates a sustained downlink jam during a ground-station pass?
questions.description: Which of the following options would be the best immediate mitigation when a downlink jam is occurring?

reason: Resetting the antenna would not help with the downlink jamming attack.
Rebooting the computer would not help with the downlink jamming attack.
Cycling the encryption key would not help with the downlink jamming attack.
Downlinking data would not help with the downlink jamming attack.
Frequency hopping would help with the downlink jamming attack by moving to an unjammed frequency.

questions.title: What is the rogue spacecraft's call-sign?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft is identified by its call-sign, visible on the spacecraft's solar panels. What is the call-sign of the rogue spacecraft?

answer.reason: The rogue asset is configured with call-sign PHANTOM on its chassis labels and mission data.

questions.title: What frequency (MHz) does the rogue spacecraft transmit its data on?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft transmits on a frequency to its ground stations. What frequency does the rogue spacecraft transmit its data on? This is not the same frequency as the jammer it has on-board, which is attempting to jam your team's frequency.

answer.reason: The rogue spacecraft transmits on frequency 905 MHz. This can be found in the EM data by analysing the spectrum of the rogue spacecraft's transmitter.

questions.title: During which ground station's pass did you experience uplink replay attacks?
questions.description: Replay traffic appears on your team's uplink frequency without a matching command from your console. When did the replay attacks occur?

reason: The spacecraft was not connected to Dubai during the replay attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Easter Island during the replay attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Miami during the replay attack.
Correct. The spacecraft was connected to Mumbai during the replay attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Paris during the replay attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Singapore during the replay attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Sydney during the replay attack.

questions.title: What action mitigates a successful uplink replay attack against your team?
questions.description: Which of the following options would be appropriate immediate mitigation when a uplink replay attack is occurring?

reason: Resetting the antenna would not help with the uplink replay attack.
Rebooting the computer would not help with the uplink replay attack.
Cycling the encryption key would help with the uplink replay attack by changing the on-board cipher.
Downlinking data would not help with the uplink replay attack.
Frequency hopping would help with the uplink replay attack by moving to an unjammed frequency.

questions.title: What fault occurred on the spacecraft storage system?
questions.description: At some point the storage system on the spacecraft was stressed. What outcome matches telemetry and capacity behaviour?

reason: The camera chain is not disabled by this Storage event.
Bit corruption would be the legacy Storage Corruption recipe; this scenario uses Storage Full instead.
Correct. The storage capacity was reduced to 100000 bytes, matching the telemetry data.
The event does not wipe stored objects.
The transmitter is unrelated to the Storage trigger.

questions.title: At what time (minutes from session start) did this fault occur?
questions.description: Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this fault first occurred.

answer.reason: This fault occurred at 1560 seconds into the simulation, and ran until the storage system was reset.

questions.title: Which reaction wheels, X, Y or Z, became stuck?
questions.description: One of the three primary reaction wheels became stuck. Which wheel became stuck? All anomalies occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

reason: Correct. This wheel became stuck, where the speed could not be changed.
Reaction Wheel Y is not stuck. The stuck condition is where the wheel speed could not be controlled.
Reaction Wheel Z is not stuck. The stuck condition is where the wheel speed could not be controlled.

questions.title: At what time (minutes from session start) did this fault occur?
questions.description: At least one of the reaction wheels became stuck. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this fault occurred. All anomalies occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

answer.reason: This fault occurred at 1620 seconds into the simulation, and ran until the reaction wheel was reset or completely nominal.

questions.title: How many green vessels are clustered in the Strait of Hormuz?
questions.description: Taking Earth observation imagery of the Hormuz region, count the number of green vessesls found.

answer.reason: Five vessels are located in the Strait of Hormuz.

questions.title: How many yellow vessels appear near near the Malacca Strait?
questions.description: Taking Earth observation imagery of the Malacca Strait region, count the number of yellow vessesls found.

answer.reason: Two yellow vessels are located near the Malacca Strait.

questions.title: At what time (in minutes from session start) did the GPS jamming event occur?
questions.description: The GPS jamming event occurred at a point in time. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this event occurred.

answer.reason: This jamming occurred at 600 seconds into the simulation.

questions.title: At what time (in minutes from session start) did the GPS jamming event stop?
questions.description: The GPS jamming event stopped at a point in time. Give elapsed simulation minutes at which this event stopped.

answer.reason: This jamming ran for 4.5 minutes, starting at 600 seconds into the simulation.

questions.title: How many distinct sustained broadcast uplink jam episodes occurred?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft held a saturating barrage jam on your team's frequency. How many unique episodes of jamming occurred? All cyber events occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

reason: Incorrect. Two episodes of jamming occurred.
Correct. One episode of jamming occurred.
Incorrect. Three episodes of jamming occurred.
Incorrect. Four episodes of jamming occurred.

questions.title: To which ground station were you connected to during the uplink jamming attack?
questions.description: The rogue spacecraft held a saturating barrage jam on your team's frequency. Which ground station(s) were you connected to during the uplink jamming attack? All cyber events occurred in the first 45 minutes of the session.

reason: The spacecraft was not connected to Dubai during the uplink jamming attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Easter Island during the uplink jamming attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Miami during the uplink jamming attack.
Correct. The spacecraft was connected to Mumbai during the uplink jamming attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Paris during the uplink jamming attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Singapore during the uplink jamming attack.
The spacecraft was not connected to Sydney during the uplink jamming attack.

questions.title: What was the lowest (non-negative) signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed during the uplink jamming attack?
questions.description: During the uplink jamming attack, what was the lowest signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed? Exclude SNR values < 5dB, including negative values.

answer.reason: The SNR dropped to 28 dB during the uplink jamming attack. This can be found in the telemetry data on the uplink connection.

questions.title: What action mitigates a sustained uplink jam during a ground-station pass?
questions.description: Which of the following options would be the best immediate mitigation when a uplink jam is occurring?

reason: Resetting the antenna would not help with the uplink jamming attack.
Rebooting the computer would not help with the uplink jamming attack.
Cycling the encryption key would not help with the downlink jamming attack.
Downlinking data would not help with the uplink jamming attack.
Frequency hopping would help with the uplink jamming attack by moving to an unjammed frequency.

questions.title: What is the Semi-Major Axis (km) of your spacecraft's orbit?
questions.description: Using on-board telemetry, calculate the semi-major axis of your spacecraft's orbit.

answer.reason: Using the GPS data, the spacecraft flies with an average altitude of 3622 km, giving a semi-major axis of 10000 km.

questions.title: What is the Inclination (deg) of your spacecraft's orbit?
questions.description: Using on-board telemetry, calculate the inclination of your spacecraft's orbit.

answer.reason: Using the GPS data, the inclination of the spacecraft's orbit is 37 degrees, found to be the maximum latitude value from the GPS data.
